data.description:
## Why
Plan-review findings #1 (T2/G5) and #2 (T3/T1) flagged that the epic's load-bearing reuse bets are unvalidated: (a) grep-ast/ctags T1-floor refutation YIELD is asserted not measured; (b) grep-ast repo-wide cross-file symbol index + (c) OpenGrep project-local rule-loading AS the registry substrate are unverified capability assertions with no spike. This de-risks them before S2/S4 lock.

## What
- Build a small polyglot fixture (Python + JS/TS + Go) with a labelled reference set (real + hallucinated symbols/imports).
- Measure: refutation yield of the T1 floor (ctags / tree-sitter tags) = fraction of REAL references positively resolved; confirm ZERO false-refute on hallucinated ones; record abstain rate.
- Determine whether grep-ast gives a repo-wide cross-file index or is seed-file-focused (the code_review.py:10 'future' concern).
- Verify OpenGrep loads project-local custom rules with metadata + emits structured (SARIF/JSON) output -> viable registry substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Refutation-yield numbers on a polyglot fixture (real-resolved %, false-refute count, abstain %).
- [ ] Verdict on grep-ast repo-wide vs seed-file; pick the floor tool accordingly.
- [ ] Verdict on OpenGrep-as-registry (custom-rule load + metadata + structured output).
- [ ] Findings recorded; design revised or confirmed.
data.title: SPIKE: validate T1-floor refutation yield (grep-ast/ctags repo-wide index) + OpenGrep-as-registry before committing S2/S4
